summary: A two-storey symmetrical courtyard building with an L-shaped red brick roof that runs along a long central corridor; exterior walls are primarily light sandstone with horizontal wood trim and stonebrick corner accents, many small horizontal windows, and white pillar-like entrance features. The structure sits inside a shallow trimmed dirt plot with a low white block border.

shape.overall_form: Two parallel rectangular wings forming a long central corridor/open courtyard between them, with a smaller detached wing at one end; roof is continuous along the long axis producing an L/C-shaped ridge when viewed from above.
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along the long central axis

elements: rows of small horizontal windows (two per level per bay)
prominent vertical white entrance pillars/door recesses
long central corridor with brick roof ridge
stonebrick corner quoins and base banding
white block low border around property
detached smaller wing at one end (mirrors main wing)
rebuild_hints: Construct two parallel 2-storey rectangular wings ~11 blocks wide with a 7-9 block gap between to form a central corridor; mirror the window and trim pattern on both wings.
Walls: primary sandstone blocks with a continuous wood plank horizontal band between floors; add stonebrick blocks at all vertical corners and a stonebrick base one block high.
Roof: use red brick blocks to create a curved/gabled roof running the full length on top of a one-block-wide stone slab parapet; roof terminates with smaller gables over the detached wing.
Entrances: place white vertical pillar features (tall 1x1 columns) at door recesses along the courtyard-facing sides; include horizontal glass panes aligned in rows for windows.
Surrounding: outline the plot with single-layer white solid blocks set on dirt/gravel ground and leave the courtyard mostly open (air) with the long corridor roof above.
uncertainties: Exact block types for white border and white vertical pillars — they appear to be quartz or white concrete; identification uncertain.
Precise internal layout and floor-to-ceiling heights are not visible; floors_estimate is based on exterior window bands.
Some small roof detail blocks (possible slabs or stairs along brick roof edges) are uncertain (slab_stone vs stone stairs).
Ground material inside the white border appears as coarse dirt or terracotta — exact block unsure.
Presence and type of doors and interior walls are not visible from provided views.
